*������������������l"""
Generic docs help button and pulse par system
"""

DocsHelper = op.TDResources.mod.DocsHelper.DocsHelper

2
*�����������������W# me - this DAT
# par - the Par object that has changed
# val - the current value
# prev - the previous value
# 
# Make sure the corresponding toggle is enabled in the Parameter Execute DAT.

def onValueChange(par, prev):
	# use par.eval() to get current value
	ext.CallbacksHelperExt.onCompParValueChange(par,prev)

# Called at end of frame with complete list of individual parameter changes.
# The changes are a list of named tuples, where each tuple is (Par, previous value)
def onValuesChanged(changes):
	for c in changes:
		# use par.eval() to get current value
		par = c.par
		prev = c.prev
	return

def onCompPulse(par):
	ext.CallbacksHelperExt.onCompParPulse(par)

def onExpressionChange(par, val, prev):
	return

def onExportChange(par, val, prev):
	return

def onEnableChange(par, val, prev):
	return

def onModeChange(par, val, prev):
	return

*�����������������K# me - this DAT
# par - the Par object that has changed
# val - the current value
# prev - the previous value
# 
# Make sure the corresponding toggle is enabled in the Parameter Execute DAT.

def onValueChange(par, prev):
	# use par.eval() to get current value
	ext.CallbacksHelperExt.onParValueChange(par,prev)

# Called at end of frame with complete list of individual parameter changes.
# The changes are a list of named tuples, where each tuple is (Par, previous value)
def onValuesChanged(changes):
	for c in changes:
		# use par.eval() to get current value
		par = c.par
		prev = c.prev
	return

def onPulse(par):
	ext.CallbacksHelperExt.onParPulse(par)

def onExpressionChange(par, val, prev):
	return

*�����������������	"""
Default callbacks

Callbacks always take a single argument. 
"""

def onTest(info : dict):
	"""
	This is a callback. Be nice to the user and document your callbacks well!

	Args;
		info: dict - The info passed to the callback. Contains the following:
			['ownerComp'] - The component that called the callback.
			['callbackName'] - The name of the callback.
			['args'] - The arguments passed to the callback.
			['kwargs'] - The keyword arguments passed to the callback.
	"""
	debug('onTest callback. info:', info)
